generated_summary: Campaign mailer sent by GOP state Senate candidate Ed Charamut. Image of Democrat Matt Lesser, who is Jewish, with large, beady eyes, holding fistfuls of hundred-dollar bills. “A new line has been crossed,” said Rep. Lesser.

Greenpeace activists scale the Shard, Europe's tallest skyscraper, as a protest against oil and gas drilling in the Arctic. The activists reached the top of the building at around 7.10pm. Police said all six had been arrested on suspicion of aggravated trespass. Greenpeace said the Shard was chosen because it was in sight of the three London bases of the energy giant Shell.

A 300-pound security robot was knocked over by a drunk man, police say. The robot was a K5 security robot, made by a company called Knightscope. Knightscope says the robot took it all in its stride and is now back on patrol. A human, 41-year-old Jason Sylvain, was charged with being drunk in public.
